faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What manufacturing risks do you review after drawings or models are submitted?
faq.a1: Our engineers review structure, material, critical dimensions and finishing needs first, focusing on thin walls, deep cavities, holes, tolerances, fixturing and cosmetic faces. If something affects stability or cost, we confirm adjustments before quoting or production.
faq.q2: What information is needed for a fast quote?
faq.a2: Provide 3D files, 2D drawings, material grade, quantity, finishing, inspection standards and target lead time. If some requirements are still open, share the use case and assembly context and we will help complete the quote inputs.
faq.q3: How are prototype and production lead times confirmed?
faq.a3: Lead time depends on process route, material availability, complexity, quantity, inspection scope and scheduling. Rush options can be evaluated separately; the confirmed quote or order schedule is final.
faq.q4: Can materials, finishing and inspection documents be packaged together?
faq.a4: Yes. We can match common metals and engineering plastics with related finishing, and provide material certificates, dimensional records or project-specific test documents. State RoHS/REACH scope before ordering when needed.
faq.q5: What if dimensional or cosmetic issues are found after delivery?
faq.a5: Keep photos, inspection data and order details. We will review drawings, acceptance criteria and production records, then arrange rework, remake, replacement or another agreed remedy.

ai.title: ProMakeHub AI Assistant
ai.aiPlaceholder: Describe part structure, material or machining needs...
ai.loginDesc: Sign in to keep using all five AI modules with project context saved.
ai.processIntro: Use this thread for process analysis of models, drawings and machining requirements. Conversations stay separate from quoting or DFM results.
ai.quoteIntro: Use this thread for standalone quote estimates. Provide material, quantity, finishing, lead time, or basics after model upload.
ai.quoteNote: Formal quotes require drawings, 3D models and quality requirements.
ai.imageIntro: Upload product photos, sketches or reference images. I will capture contours and key features, then list dimensions still needed.
ai.imageNote: Results can continue into engineering review, DFM and quoting.
ai.textIntro: Describe use, shape, size and structure in natural language. I will break requirements into confirmable modeling parameters.
ai.textNote: More specific descriptions produce results closer to manufacturing needs.
ai.dfmIntro: Use this thread for standalone DFM advice, focusing on wall thickness, holes, chamfers, assembly gaps and finishing risks.
ai.dfmNote: Add critical sizes, tolerance grades and cosmetic faces before formal production.

capabilitiesHome.docTitle: Capabilities Center | Processes, Services, Materials and Finishes | ProMakeHub
capabilitiesHome.heroDesc: Explore ProMakeHub manufacturing capabilities across processes, services, materials and surface finishing, then choose the right page for your part geometry, performance goals and delivery stage.
capabilitiesHome.sectionDesc: Start from the question your project needs to answer: which process fits the geometry, which service supports delivery, which material meets performance, or which finish improves function and appearance.
capabilitiesHome.processDesc: Review machining, cutting, forming and additive manufacturing routes for different part structures.
capabilitiesHome.serviceDesc: Compare prototype, custom manufacturing, forming, inspection and delivery support by project stage.
capabilitiesHome.materialDesc: Compare metals, engineering plastics and composites by strength, weight, corrosion resistance and cost.
capabilitiesHome.finishDesc: Choose finishing options around appearance, corrosion resistance, wear resistance, insulation and marking.
capabilitiesHome.faqDesc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

faq.q: What projects is Capabilities Center suitable for?
faq.a: Capabilities Center is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

faq.q: What information is needed for a Capabilities Center quote?
faq.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

faq.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Capabilities Center?
faq.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

capabilitiesHome.ctaTitle: Need help matching a manufacturing route?
capabilitiesHome.ctaDesc: Submit drawings, models or project requirements and engineers can review process, material, finishing and quality needs together.

capabilities.heroDesc: A variety of advanced processes to meet full-process manufacturing needs from prototype to mass production
capabilities.sectionDesc: Covers a variety of precision machining processes, supports prototype development to mass production, and meets the high-precision parts manufacturing needs of different industries.
capabilities.tipBody: Use sheet processes for housings and brackets, CNC or complex machining for curved geometry, 3D printing or urethane casting for low-volume validation, and injection molding for production plastic parts.
